title: Build a Real-World Multi-Agent AI App with MCP + LangGraph | Part 2
summary: Tutorial video on building a multi-agent travel planning system by integrating LangGraph agents with MCP servers for real-time flight and weather data. The application combines LangGraph, Model Context Protocol, PostgreSQL for memory, Tavily Search, AviationStack API, OpenWeatherMap API, and Streamlit UI.
writeup: Part 2 of Code With Aarohi's series walks through building a multi-agent travel planning application that connects LangGraph agents to MCP servers for real-time flight and weather data. It matters as a concrete demonstration of how the Model Context Protocol wires external tools and APIs into an agent workflow rather than treating them as abstractions. The stack pairs LangGraph and MCP with PostgreSQL for memory, Tavily Search, the AviationStack API for flights, the OpenWeatherMap API for weather, and a Streamlit UI. The result is a working example of multi-agent coordination with persistent memory and live data sources.
